cross_connections.display_label: Half-sibling of Isabella Beecher Hooker through Lyman Beecher
cross_connections.description: Edward and Isabella were half-siblings through Lyman Beecher — Edward by Roxanna Foote (first wife), Isabella by Harriet Porter (second wife). Both committed to reform.

cross_connections.display_label: Brother of Harriet Beecher Stowe — Roxanna Foote's children
cross_connections.description: Both children of Lyman Beecher through Roxanna Foote (first wife) — Harriet the novelist, Edward the abolitionist college president.

narrative_blocks.header: He led Illinois College and fought slavery
narrative_blocks.body: Edward Beecher was the first president of Illinois College and helped organize the state's first antislavery society. He stood close to the abolitionist editor Elijah Lovejoy in the months before Lovejoy's murder.

narrative_blocks.header: He was born into the famous Beecher pulpit
narrative_blocks.body: Son of the revivalist Lyman Beecher, he was a full brother of Harriet Beecher Stowe and a half-brother of Isabella Beecher Hooker. The clan's reforming zeal ran through his own long ministry.

INST022.primary_name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.name: American Board of Commissioners for Foreign Missions
INST022.notes: First American foreign-mission organization. Tree connections: Rev. Elias Cornelius (agent 1816, secretary 1831-32); Ainsworth and Harriet Ellsworth Blunt, missionaries to the Cherokee at Brainerd, Tennessee; Eliza Talcott, missionary to Japan; Rev. William Banfield Capron, Madura mission; Judge John Hooker, member.
